primary.en: After a heavy meal

secondary.en: Helps ease bloating and supports digestion.

primary.en: Mid-afternoon, as a snack

secondary.tr: Tok tutar ve bağırsak florasını destekler.
secondary.en: Keeps you full and supports gut flora.

primary.en: About 15 minutes after eating

secondary.tr: Kan şekerinin daha dengeli seyretmesine yardımcı olur.
secondary.en: Helps keep blood sugar steadier after meals.

primary.en: Between meals when you feel bloated

secondary.tr: Mideye yumuşak, yatıştırıcı bir mola sunar.
secondary.en: Offers the stomach a gentle, settling pause.

primary.en: Alongside your meals

secondary.tr: Bağırsak florasını destekleyen tanıdık bir seçim.
secondary.en: A familiar choice that supports gut flora.

primary.en: At every meal, for the first few bites

secondary.tr: Sindirim ağızda başlar; tokluğu fark etmen kolaylaşır.
secondary.en: Digestion starts in the mouth and fullness is easier to notice.

primary.tr: Ağır bir öğünden sonra, küçük yudumlarla
primary.en: After a heavy meal, in small sips

secondary.en: Feels soothing to a full stomach.

primary.en: On evenings when you feel full

secondary.tr: Ferah bir his verir, mideyi yatıştırır.
secondary.en: Brings a fresh feeling and settles the stomach.

primary.en: In the morning, on an empty stomach

secondary.tr: Güne hafif bir başlangıç ve sıvı alımı için keyifli bir yol.
secondary.en: A pleasant way to hydrate and ease into the day.

primary.en: While cooking winter meals

secondary.en: Adds a traditional depth to meals.

primary.en: On cold mornings

secondary.tr: Güne tatlı ve sıcak bir başlangıç.
secondary.en: A warm, sweet way to start the day.

primary.en: At breakfast or as a snack

secondary.tr: Mevsiminde taze ve canlandırıcı bir seçim.
secondary.en: Fresh and refreshing when in season.

primary.en: On chilly evenings

secondary.tr: Isıtır ve seni yavaşlamaya davet eder.
secondary.en: Warms you and invites you to slow down.

primary.en: When your body signals it

secondary.tr: Dinlenmek, kendine bakmanın en basit yoludur.
secondary.en: Rest is the simplest form of self-care.

primary.en: A few minutes morning and evening

secondary.en: Fresh air keeps the space pleasant.

primary.en: After coming home and before eating

secondary.tr: Basit bir alışkanlık, günlük bir özen.
secondary.en: A simple habit, an everyday kindness.

primary.en: About an hour before bed

secondary.tr: Sakinleşmeye ve güne yumuşak bir kapanış yapmaya yardımcı olur.
secondary.en: Helps you wind down for a calmer end to the day.

primary.en: 30–60 minutes before bed

secondary.tr: Zihnin yavaşlamasına ve uykuya geçişin kolaylaşmasına yardımcı olur.
secondary.en: Lets the mind slow down so falling asleep gets easier.

primary.en: In the hours before bed

secondary.en: Reminds your body the day is ending.

primary.en: Before sleep

secondary.en: A cool room makes resting easier.

primary.en: From late afternoon onward

secondary.en: Gives your evening mind room to settle.

primary.en: As much as you can through the week

secondary.tr: Düzen, uykuya geçişi tahmin edilebilir kılar.
secondary.en: Routine makes falling asleep more predictable.

primary.en: Before bed, when your mind is full

secondary.en: Letting thoughts onto paper feels relieving.

primary.en: In bed, eyes closed

secondary.tr: Yavaş nefes bedeni dinlenmeye davet eder.
secondary.en: Slow breathing invites the body to rest.

primary.en: Within the first hour of waking

secondary.tr: Vücut saatini düzenler, gün içi uyanıklığı destekler.
secondary.en: Helps set your body clock and supports daytime alertness.

primary.en: After getting up in the morning

secondary.en: Gently wakes the body up.

primary.en: As you start the day

secondary.en: A balanced start carries your morning.

primary.en: In the middle of a long task

secondary.en: Short breaks refresh your focus.

primary.en: During your lunch break

secondary.en: Movement and air lift your energy.

primary.en: When you feel sluggish

secondary.en: Sometimes tiredness is really thirst.

primary.en: When your energy dips

secondary.tr: Tanıdık bir melodi ruh hâlini yükseltir.
secondary.en: A familiar tune lifts your mood.

primary.en: After a long time indoors

secondary.tr: Bir anlık temiz hava zihni açar.
secondary.en: A moment of fresh air clears the mind.

primary.en: At regular intervals

secondary.tr: Cilde iyi gelen en basit alışkanlık.
secondary.en: The simplest habit your skin appreciates.

primary.en: Before going out during the day

secondary.en: Shields your skin from the sun all day.

primary.en: Morning and evening

secondary.en: Refreshes skin without stressing it.

primary.en: Right after washing

secondary.en: Moisture keeps skin soft.

primary.en: Every night

secondary.tr: Dinlenmiş bir cilt daha canlı görünür.
secondary.en: Rested skin looks more alive.

primary.en: While showering

secondary.tr: Çok sıcak yerine ılık su cilde daha naziktir.
secondary.en: Lukewarm over very hot water is kinder to skin.

primary.en: In your meals

secondary.en: Color on your plate shows on your skin.

primary.en: Through the day, without noticing

secondary.tr: Küçük bir farkındalık cildi rahat bırakır.
secondary.en: A little awareness gives skin a break.

primary.en: Right after waking, before breakfast

secondary.tr: Gece boyu kaybedilen sıvıyı yerine koyar, güne canlı başlatır.
secondary.en: Replaces fluid lost overnight and helps you start the day fresh.

primary.en: When leaving home

secondary.en: Visible water reminds you to drink.

primary.en: Shortly before a meal

secondary.en: Helps you come to the table calmer.

primary.en: When plain water feels dull

secondary.en: A pleasant way to take in fluids.

primary.en: As snacks

secondary.tr: Hem serinletir hem sıvı katkısı sağlar.
secondary.en: Refreshes and adds to your fluids.

primary.en: After exercise or a walk

secondary.en: Replaces the fluid you used up.

primary.en: When cold days make water unappealing

secondary.en: Warm water makes sipping easier.

primary.en: Throughout the day

secondary.en: Listening to your body is the best guide.

title.tr: "Sana yardım ederim ama senin yerine yapmam."
title.en: "I'll help you, but I won't do it for you."

primary.tr: Çocuk bir işte zorlanıp pes etmek üzereyken
primary.en: When your child struggles and is about to give up

secondary.en: Offers support while leaving responsibility with the child.

title.tr: "Şu an durduruyorum çünkü güvende kalman gerekiyor."
title.en: "I'm stopping this now because you need to stay safe."

primary.en: When you need to stop a risky behavior

secondary.tr: Sınırın amacının kontrol değil, korumak olduğunu gösterir.
secondary.en: Shows the boundary is about protection, not control.

title.tr: "Benim işim seni korumak, her dediğine evet demek değil."
title.en: "My job is to keep you safe, not to say yes to everything."

primary.en: When facing repeated pushback on a 'no'

secondary.tr: Ebeveynliğin amacının memnun etmek değil rehberlik etmek olduğunu hatırlatır.
secondary.en: Reminds that parenting is about guidance, not pleasing.

title.en: "I hear you, but my decision won't change."

primary.en: When your child tries to negotiate a firm limit

secondary.tr: Sınırın tartışmaya açık olmadığını sakin bir dille ifade eder.
secondary.en: Calmly signals the limit isn't up for debate.

primary.en: When every limit is met with 'why'

secondary.tr: Bir sınırın geçerli olması için uzun gerekçe gerekmediğini gösterir.
secondary.en: Shows a limit doesn't need a long justification to be valid.

primary.en: When your child wants control but the rule stays

secondary.en: Offers freedom inside the boundary.

primary.en: When the same request keeps coming back

secondary.en: Calm repetition gives clarity without a fight.

primary.en: When a request needs to wait

secondary.tr: Beklemeyi cezasız, net bir sıraya bağlar.
secondary.en: Frames waiting as a clear order, not a punishment.

title.tr: "İstediğin gibi olmadığı için üzülmen çok normal."
title.en: "It's okay to be upset that it didn't go your way."

primary.en: When your child is disappointed

secondary.tr: Duyguyu kabul eder ama sınırdan vazgeçmez.
secondary.en: Accepts the feeling without abandoning the boundary.

primary.en: When your child can't name what they feel

secondary.en: Naming a feeling makes it feel understood.

title.en: "I'm here, I'll stay until it passes."

primary.en: When your child is crying or overwhelmed

secondary.en: Your presence offers a safe harbor.

title.en: "All feelings are okay; not all actions are."

primary.tr: Öfke bir şeye zarar vermeye dönüştüğünde
primary.en: When anger turns into hurting something

secondary.tr: Duyguyu kabul ederken davranışa sınır koyar.
secondary.en: Accepts the feeling while limiting the action.

title.en: "We can pause for a bit if you want."

primary.en: When feelings run very high

secondary.en: A pause makes room to settle.

title.en: "Can you tell me a little more?"

primary.tr: Çocuk bir şeye üzülüp içine kapandığında
primary.en: When your child shuts down over something

secondary.en: Curiosity opens the door to talking.

title.en: "That was a hard day, wasn't it?"

primary.tr: Yorgun ve gergin bir günün sonunda
primary.en: At the end of a tiring, tense day

secondary.en: A shared observation lightens the load.

title.en: "I saw you try hard to calm down."

primary.en: When your child works to pull themselves together

secondary.en: Noticing the effort builds them up.

primary.en: When your child avoids a task

secondary.tr: İş birliğini emir vermeden davet eder.
secondary.en: Invites cooperation without issuing commands.

primary.en: When starting a task feels hard

secondary.en: A choice makes cooperation feel voluntary.

primary.en: When there's a mess to clean up

secondary.tr: Oyun, görevi hafif ve eğlenceli yapar.
secondary.en: Play makes a chore light and fun.

title.en: "When it's tidy, we can go to the park."

primary.tr: Bir işi keyifli bir şeye bağlamak istediğinde
primary.en: When linking a task to something fun

secondary.en: Sets a clear order and reduces bargaining.

primary.tr: Çocuğu bir işe dâhil etmek istediğinde
primary.en: When you want to involve your child

secondary.en: Feeling needed makes contributing feel worthwhile.

primary.en: When moving from one activity to another

secondary.en: A heads-up softens the transition.

title.en: "Can you show me how it's done?"

primary.en: When your child feels capable at something

secondary.tr: Rol vermek iş birliğini davet eder.
secondary.en: Giving a role invites cooperation.

title.en: "I really liked how you set the table."

primary.en: Right after your child helps

secondary.en: Specific thanks reinforces the behavior.

title.tr: "Bu zor ama senin bunu yapabileceğini biliyorum."
title.en: "This is hard, but I know you can handle it."

primary.en: When your child says 'I can't'

secondary.tr: Zorluğu küçümsemeden çocuğun yeterlilik duygusunu besler.
secondary.en: Builds competence without dismissing the difficulty.

primary.tr: Sonuç ne olursa olsun, çaba sonrası
primary.en: After the effort, whatever the result

secondary.en: Praising effort builds resilience.

primary.en: When your child is upset over a mistake

secondary.en: Normalizing mistakes makes trying easier.

primary.en: When your child manages something alone

secondary.en: Asking about the process deepens ownership.

title.en: "Try first; I'm here if you need me."

primary.en: When your child asks for help right away

secondary.en: Makes room to try and grows confidence.

title.en: "Last time was hard too, and then you did it."

primary.en: When your child loses heart

secondary.en: Past success becomes today's courage.

title.en: "That was your idea — I love it."

primary.en: When your child offers an idea

secondary.en: Valuing their ideas nurtures initiative.

primary.en: When an attempt doesn't quite work

secondary.en: Pairs trying again with encouragement.

primary.en: When a young child is mid-meltdown

secondary.tr: Duyguyu isimlendirmek çocuğun sakinleşmesine yardımcı olur.
secondary.en: Naming the feeling helps the child settle.

primary.en: When telling a toddler what comes next

secondary.tr: Kısa ve sıralı cümleler anlaşılır olur.
secondary.en: Short, ordered phrases are easier to follow.

primary.en: While doing everyday routines

secondary.en: Narrating builds vocabulary.

primary.en: When a young child struggles with something

secondary.en: Being noticed helps them keep going.

primary.en: As the bedtime routine nears

secondary.en: A gentle heads-up eases the transition.

primary.en: When your child touches too roughly

secondary.en: Saying what to do works better than 'don't'.

primary.en: When a young child is scared

secondary.en: Calm reassurance soothes the body.

primary.en: At drop-off or goodbye

secondary.en: A consistent goodbye builds trust.
